description: Alpine shared regulatory catalogue — canonical federal / PR-local / NCQA authorities governing Puerto Rico Medicaid (Plan Vital) health-plan operations. Consumed by all Alpine apps.

authorities.title: Medicaid Managed Care
authorities.summary: Governing federal regulation for Medicaid managed care contracts (MCO/PIHP/PAHP). Subpart E is the quality core. Applies to PR territories.

authorities.title: State monitoring requirements
authorities.summary: State must monitor all managed-care programs: enrollee services, grievances/appeals, network adequacy, quality, financials, encounter data, and use of measures/PIPs; annual monitoring + readiness review.

authorities.title: Health information systems
authorities.summary: MCO must maintain a health information system that collects, analyzes, integrates and reports data; ensures encounter-data accuracy and completeness. Basis for IS/data-integration duties.

authorities.title: Quality Assessment & Performance Improvement (QAPI)
authorities.summary: MCO must run an ongoing QAPI: collect/submit performance-measure data, conduct PIPs, detect under/over-utilization, and address enrollees with special health-care needs.

authorities.title: Managed care State quality strategy
authorities.summary: State must maintain a written quality strategy (measures, targets, PIPs, disparity reduction, EQR arrangements); public comment; review ≥ every 3 years.

authorities.title: External Quality Review (EQR)
authorities.summary: State must ensure a qualified EQRO performs an annual EQR of each contracting MCO/PIHP.

authorities.title: Activities related to EQR
authorities.summary: Mandatory EQR activities: validation of performance measures, validation of PIPs, compliance review (every 3 yrs), and network-adequacy validation; plus optional activities.

authorities.title: EQR results / annual technical report
authorities.summary: State must produce an annual public EQR technical report on access/quality with plan-specific strengths, weaknesses, recommendations; posted by April 30.

authorities.title: Medicaid/CHIP Quality Rating System (MAC QRS)
authorities.summary: Mandatory Quality Rating System framework, mandatory measure set, methodology, and public one-stop-shop display (2024 rule).

authorities.title: Network adequacy standards
authorities.summary: Time/distance and other network-adequacy standards; validated as a mandatory EQR activity (2023 protocol).

authorities.title: Information requirements for enrollees
authorities.summary: Enrollee information standards including language access (Spanish for PR Medicaid) and alternate formats.

authorities.title: Mental health / substance use parity
authorities.summary: Applies MHPAEA parity to Medicaid managed care (financial requirements, treatment limitations, NQTLs).

authorities.title: Mandatory Medicaid/CHIP Core Set reporting
authorities.summary: Mandatory annual reporting of the Child Core Set and behavioral-health Adult measures in a standardized format.

authorities.title: EPSDT — Early & Periodic Screening, Diagnostic & Treatment
authorities.summary: Child preventive-services benefit; CMS-416 annual reporting.

authorities.title: Child Core Set mandatory (BBA 2018)
authorities.summary: Bipartisan Budget Act of 2018 makes annual Child Core Set reporting mandatory beginning FFY 2024.

authorities.title: Behavioral-health Adult measures mandatory
authorities.summary: Makes BH measures of the Adult Core Set mandatory beginning FFY 2024; remaining adult measures voluntary.

authorities.title: 2024 Managed Care "Access, Finance & Quality" Final Rule
authorities.summary: Establishes MAC QRS (Subpart G), adds network-adequacy EQR validation, transparency; effective Jul 9, 2024.

authorities.title: Mandatory Medicaid/CHIP Core Set Reporting rule
authorities.summary: Implements mandatory annual Child Core Set + BH Adult measure reporting mechanics and phased content.

authorities.title: Initial Core Set Mandatory Reporting Guidance
authorities.summary: State Health Official letter confirming Child Core Set + BH Adult measures mandatory starting FFY 2024.

authorities.title: External Quality Review Protocols
authorities.summary: Standardized methods for each EQR activity (PIP validation, PM validation, compliance, network adequacy).

authorities.title: Medicaid Managed Care Final Rules (2016, 2020)
authorities.summary: Created the modern QAPI/quality-strategy/EQR architecture (2016) and rebalanced oversight/flexibility (2020).

authorities.title: Territory Medicaid funding cap / FMAP
authorities.summary: PR Medicaid funded via annual federal cap plus enhanced funds (FMAP 76% through 9/30/2027); does not reduce Part 438 quality duties.

authorities.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule
authorities.summary: PHI use/disclosure, minimum necessary, Business Associate requirements.

authorities.title: HIPAA Security Rule — technical safeguards
authorities.summary: Access control, audit controls, integrity, and transmission security for ePHI. Basis for the app's audit trail.

authorities.title: HIPAA Transactions & Code Sets (X12)
authorities.summary: Standard EDI transactions (837, 835, 999, 277CA) and code sets used for claims/encounters/remittances.

authorities.title: Ley de la Administración de Seguros de Salud (ASES)
authorities.summary: Creates ASES/PRHIA — the public corporation that contracts, supervises and evaluates the MCOs delivering Plan Vital. Statutory basis for all quality/reporting obligations.

authorities.title: First Medical Plan Vital MCO Contract (2023–2025)
authorities.summary: The operative MCO instrument: covered benefits, capitation (HCHN carve-in), encounter data, quality/HEDIS reporting (§12, §18.2), grievances/appeals, network adequacy.

authorities.citation: PR Medicaid Managed Care Quality Strategy (10-21-24)
authorities.title: PR Medicaid & CHIP Managed Care Quality Strategy
authorities.summary: CMS-required §438.340 strategy: required HEDIS measure set, HCIP withhold, PIP topics, CAHPS/ECHO, EQRO protocols. The single best authoritative map.

authorities.title: Plan Vital Reporting Guide
authorities.summary: Catalogue of MCO reports/formats/frequencies/deadlines: Report 24 Audited HEDIS, 26 Core Measures, 22 HCIP (qtrly), 13 CMS-416 EPSDT, 12 Encounter Data.

authorities.title: Required HEDIS measure set (ASES policy letter)
authorities.summary: ASES normative letter communicating the required HEDIS measure set (per HEDIS MY Vol. 2) captured in Report 24. Binding policy guidance.

authorities.title: Health Care Improvement Program (quality withhold)
authorities.summary: Quality withhold: ~2% of the admin fee earned back quarterly on benchmark targets across Healthy People, Chronic Conditions, and a third initiative. Reported via Report 22.

authorities.title: PR External Quality Review Annual Technical Report
authorities.summary: Mercer (EQRO since 2022) validates PIPs, performance measures and HEDIS for the GHP MCOs incl. FMHP; demonstrates §438.350–.364 compliance.

authorities.title: APR DRG inpatient pricing readiness attestation
authorities.summary: Go-live Oct 1, 2025: inpatient DRG pricing (Solventum) — P&Ps, coding support, system testing, CARC/RARC, encounter→MMIS. Tracked in the Readiness Tracker.

authorities.title: Código de Seguros — Pago Puntual / Clean Claims
authorities.summary: Clean-claim + prompt-payment mandate: 30-day payment window, 90-day provider filing, 15-day deficiency notice, interest on late pay. Applies to MCOs (HSOs).

authorities.title: Código de Seguros — Organizaciones de Servicios de Salud (HSO)
authorities.summary: Licensing/solvency framework for Health Service Organizations — the corporate form MCOs operate under, regulated by OCS.

authorities.title: Prompt-pay / clean-claim amendments
authorities.summary: Establish and tighten prompt-payment: standardized clean-claim definition, uniform 30-day rule, telemedicine-as-in-person, Art. 30.040 amendments.

authorities.title: Código de Seguros de Salud de PR
authorities.summary: Health-insurance consumer code: member protections, coverage definitions, internal/external appeals, transparency.

authorities.title: Carta de Derechos y Responsabilidades del Paciente
authorities.summary: Patient bill of rights binding on health plans: quality care, non-discrimination, 24/7 ER access without prior auth, privacy, informed consent, continuity of care.

authorities.title: Ley de Salud Mental de PR
authorities.summary: Mental-health code: MH Bill of Rights, service procedures, consent, and managed-care alignment. Foundational for BH measures and member rights.

authorities.title: OCS — Insurance Commissioner authority & reglamentos
authorities.summary: Enforces the Insurance Code, licenses insurers/HSOs, sets clean-claim/prompt-pay standards, adjudicates non-payment complaints.

authorities.title: Ley de Transparencia y Acceso a la Información Pública
authorities.summary: PR public-records / FOIA-equivalent — how to obtain ASES/OCS public records (contracts, quality strategies, reglamentos).

authorities.title: Technical Specifications for Health Plans
authorities.summary: Per-measure technical spec: eligible population, numerator/denominator, exclusions, admin/hybrid logic, sampling. Required to compute/submit HEDIS. Annual, licensed.

authorities.title: Value Set Directory
authorities.summary: Code tables (CPT/HCPCS/LOINC/ICD-10/SNOMED) crosswalked to each measure. Licensed, per MY.

authorities.title: Compliance Audit — Standards, Policies & Procedures
authorities.summary: The only NCQA-approved audit methodology: IS capabilities (IS 1–7), measure-compliance evaluation, MRRV, result designations. Licensed.

authorities.title: HEDIS Compliance Audit program (IS 1–7)
authorities.summary: Two-part audit (IS capabilities + measure review) by a Certified HEDIS Compliance Auditor; required before rates are reportable.

authorities.title: Measure Certification (test decks)
authorities.summary: Per-measure, per-year certification: run NCQA test decks through the engine; NCQA compares to expected results and issues a seal.

authorities.title: Digital HEDIS measure packages (FHIR + CQL)
authorities.summary: Official machine-readable HEDIS measures in FHIR/CQL for direct execution (the engine's measure logic). Subscription.

authorities.title: Electronic Clinical Data Systems reporting
authorities.summary: Full-population, non-sampled reporting from structured electronic clinical data via FHIR; replaces hybrid as measures go digital.

authorities.title: DAV & supplemental-data / PSV rules
authorities.summary: Validated data streams count as standard supplemental data (skip PSV); non-standard data requires Primary Source Verification.

authorities.title: CAHPS / ECHO survey administration
authorities.summary: Plan must use an NCQA-Certified survey vendor for CAHPS (Adult/Child) and ECHO (behavioral health); cannot self-administer.

authorities.title: IDSS submission & Patient-Level Data (PLD)
authorities.summary: Web submission of audited HEDIS results (IDSS) and member-level detail (PLD) on the annual timeline.

authorities.title: Hybrid retirement (MY2029) & all-digital (MY2030)
authorities.summary: Proposed timeline: hybrid method retired by MY2029; HEDIS fully digital (FHIR/CQL) by MY2030. Drives ECDS/digital readiness.

authorities.title: FHIR/CQL standards for digital HEDIS
authorities.summary: HL7 FHIR R4 + Clinical Quality Language, per the HEDIS FHIR Implementation Guide — the technical basis of the digital engine.
